document_metadata.title: Website to Facebook Messenger Custom Integration

executive_summary: Following the deprecation of the Meta Customer Chat Plugin in May 2024, this project outlines the creation of a custom, API-first web chat widget. The interface will sync two-way communication between website visitors and a Facebook Page Inbox utilizing the Meta Graph API.
access_prerequisites: Manage access to the target Facebook Page
Meta App created with the 'Messenger' product enabled
Generated Page Access Token (PAT) with 'pages_messaging' and 'pages_show_list' permissions

architecture_strategy.frontend_client: Custom floating chat UI built with React-TypeScript / Next.js for a seamless, branded user experience.
architecture_strategy.middleware_layer: A NestJS backend or an n8n webhook workflow to securely store tokens, pass Meta's webhook verification, and route payloads.

functional_requirements: FR1: The system must render a toggleable floating chat window on the website.
FR2: The system must capture user text inputs and send them to the middleware.
FR3: The middleware must transmit outgoing messages to the Meta Graph API.
FR4: The system must receive and display real-time replies from the Facebook Page inbox via Meta Webhooks.
FR5: The client must handle guest user session IDs (e.g., via localStorage) to maintain conversation state without requiring a Facebook login.
FR6: The backend must strictly respond to Meta's 'hub.challenge' to validate the webhook connection.

execution_milestones.name: Meta Developer Environment Configuration
tasks: Create a new Business App in the Meta Developer Console.
Link the managed Facebook Page to the application.
Generate the Page Access Token and save it to the middleware environment variables.

execution_milestones.name: Middleware & Webhook Construction
tasks: Create a GET endpoint (NestJS or n8n) to handle Meta's initial webhook verification request (hub.mode, hub.verify_token, hub.challenge).
Configure the Webhook URL in the Meta Developer Console and subscribe to 'messages' fields.
Create a POST endpoint to receive asynchronous message payloads from Meta.
Create a secure internal API endpoint for the website frontend to trigger outgoing messages.

execution_milestones.name: Frontend Widget Engineering
tasks: Develop the Next.js / React-TypeScript chat UI components (message bubbles, input field, toggle button).
Implement state management for the live conversation thread.
Connect the frontend input field to the internal middleware POST endpoint.
Implement a polling mechanism or WebSocket connection to fetch incoming replies from the middleware to the UI.

execution_milestones.name: Testing, Security & Deployment
tasks: Ensure no Meta tokens or secrets are exposed in the client-side code.
Conduct end-to-end testing: Send a message from the website UI and reply from the Meta Business Suite inbox.
Handle API rate limits and standard error responses gracefully in the UI.
Deploy the middleware to production and embed the widget into the live website.
